mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 1.0 percentage point signals abrupt labor market deterioration and recession onset, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during supply chain disruption periods, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation rate exceeding 0.3% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >65% of high-inflation months, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when GDP growth falls below 1.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product reflect underlying economic expansion that translates into higher corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in >60% of quarters, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% indicates severe supply-side contraction, forcing equity repricing and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge over random baseline in out-of-sample tests.
